(2) It shall come into force on the date of the publication in the {)flicial Gazette. Dcl’initon. 2. In this Scheme, unless the context otherwise requires:— *(a) “Act” means the Bhopal Gas Leak Disaster (Processing of Claims) Act, 1985 1 (21 of1985); *(aa) “Bhopal Gas thims Monthly Unit Scheme” means the i “Bhopal Gas Victims Monthly Unit Scheme, 1992” made by the Board of the i unitTcust of India in exercise of the powers com-"erred by the Section 21 of , i the unitTrustofIndia Act,1963,(53 of 1963); (b) “Section” means a Section of the Act ; (0) words and expressions used herein and not defined but defined in the Act, shall have the meanings respectively assigned. to them in the Act. (1) An a‘pplication for claim shall be made to the Deputy Gommissimier iiiiilmg clalm‘g‘ cOncerned in Form No. 1 or,as the case may be, Form No. 2 or Form No. 3 or Form NO- 4 or Form No.5appended tothis Scheme within. a period ofsixty days from the date notified by the 00mmissioner inviting applications for claims: Provided that if the Deputy CommissiOner is satisfied that the ciaimant was presen ted by Sufficient cause from filing the application for the claims W1 thin the sxad lperiod -i of sixty days, he may entertain the application for chaplains within afurther'period ofsixty days but not thereafter. (2) Not W1 thstanding anything contained in sub-paragraph (J!) an application for a claim arising in future On account of the BhOpal gas leak disaster may be made to the Deputy Commissioner within such further period, after the expiry .of the period 3pacified in the provisiOn to the said sub-paragraph (l) as he may specify from time to time. (3) The claim may be made by any person attested by the Bhopal Gas Leak Disas- tci' or,as the case may be, the spouse, children or other heirs of such deceased perspnor bya represen tativc duly authorised by such person in this behalf or. in the case or a. minor so affected, by his guardian. (4) A separate claim form in respect of each category of claim specified in paragrape 5 of the Scheme shall be filed by each person havmga claim. Each person filinga claim shall be corisidcredto have a single claim regardless of the number of nategories of claim included therein. ‘ (5) The Deputy Commissioner shall provide the required forms for filing the ‘ applications free of cost. ‘ WWW!“ or I db4 A. Categories, namely ; (a) death; . ' i1 (b) total disablement resulting in permanent disability to earn livelihood. ‘ (c) permanent partial dirablement aflecting the overall capacity to earn his livelihood ; 1 (d) temporary partial disablement resulting in reduced capacity to earn livelihood ‘ '3(da) injury of Utmost severity; i=(db) minory injury ; (e) temporary dislocation of means of livelihood; (5) claims of the Government, authorities under the control of the Government, local authorities and institutuitms for expenses incurred in providing relief aid and. rehabilitation to the II person airlected by the BhOpal Gas Leak Disaster ; (g) administration expenses incurred by the Central Government, Government of Madhya Pradesh or local authorities to cope up with the Bhopal Gas Leak disaster, including all I local and administrativae expenses attributable or related the said disastr r; (it) claims relating to loss of revenue to Government authorities under the control of Govern- l ment or local authori ties arising out of, or connected. wi th Bhopal Gas Leah Disaster; (l) claims on account of damage to the fauna including mi lch and drought animals; (j) claims arising from damage to flora including destruction of agricultural crops, vegetables, trees and orchards; (it) claims on account of damage to environment including pollution of soil, flora, fauna and water systrms; (1) claims relating to loss and distructicr cf prorri t}; (In) claims relating to loss of business of employment or both; (in) clgims in respect ofinjuries that are likely to be suffered on account of the B11 _ a1 GasLeak isaster; (0) any other claim. or claims which the Deputy Commissioner may determine for reasons to I, be recorded in writingas arising out of, or connected wi th5 ti (_ I l c; 2.] {as It a]: disaster. (3) on the consideration of aclaim made under paragraph. 4 ol the SC] in; , if the Deputy Commi- ssiOner is of the opinion that the claim falls in a catergory difi'erentfrom the category mentionc d by the claimant, he may decide the appropriate category after giving an oppourtuni ty to the claimant to be heard and alsoafter takingin to comidera ti on an yfa cts made available tohi min this behalf by theGovem- mentin this behalf. ‘ (4) Where the Deputy Commissioner is of the opinion that a claim made under paragraph 4 does notfallin any ofthe caterfories specifiedin sub-paragraph (2) he may refuse to register the claim; Provided that before so refusing, he shall give a reasonable opportunity for a personalhearing to the clailnan t. (5) If the claimantisnot satisfied with the order of the Deputy Commissioner under sub-paragaph (3) or sub-paragraph (4) he may prefer an appeal against such order to the Additional Commissmner who shall decide the same. ‘ ' l (6) Every appeal under sub-paragraph (5) shall be filed in such Form as may be speCified by the- Commissioner within 60 days from the date-on which the order sought to be appealed againstls commu- nicated to the calimant preferring the appeal. The CommissiOner shall have the pOWer to regulate his OWn procedure, and the Procedure. procedure to be followed by the Deputy Comm' ssi Oner and other officers and employees appo- inted under section 6,in all maters arising out of discharge ofhis or, as the case may be, their functions under this scheme, includfrigthe place or places where he shallhold his sittings. ‘ 9. There shall be created and maintained by the Central Government a Fund to be Processing of called the Processing of Claims Account Fund. . Claims of Acc0_ ‘ l (2) The Fund shall include the amount wich the Central Government may unt Fund. after due appropriation made by Parliament by lawin that behalf, credit to the said Fund and any other amounts which may be credited to such Fund. . ‘ (3) The amount or as the case may be, the amounts in the said Fund shall be applied by the Commissioner for meeting expenses in connectiOn with administration of this Scheme and of the provisions of the Act. ‘ 10. (1) There shall be created and maintained by the Central Governmenta Fund Claims and to be called the Claims and Relief Fund. ‘ Relief Fund | (2) The Fund shallinclude the amounts received in satisfaction of the claims and any other amounts made avaiable to the CommissiOner as donatiOn of relief purposes. tin (3) The amountsin the said Fund shall be applied by the Commissioner for the flowing purposes, namely :——- . (a) disbursal of amounts in settlement of claims registered with the Deputy Com- missioners ; (b) disbursal of amounts as relief (including interim relief) to the persons afiec. ted by the Bhopal gas leak disaster ; and (C) apportionment of part of the fund for disbursal of amounts in settlement of claims arising in future or for disbursal of amounts to the Government of . i Madhya Pradesh for the social and economic rehabilitation of the persons ‘ afiected by the Bhopal gas leak disaster. (4) Scperate accounts shall be maintained for receipt and disbursal of’ amounts in‘ settlement of claims and for relief purp0ses. l l. (3) The Deputy Commissioner shall determine the quantum of compensation payable to each claimant within a category specifiedin paragraph 5 in accordance with the provisi ens of sub-paragraph (4) subject to any Court order, settlement or award of damages in any specific case. - (4) In determining the quantum of compensatiOn payable to the claimants. within different categories specified in paragraph 5, regard shall be had amOngstl other factors, to the following factors, namely :— (a) the probable life span of the person aflected by the Bhopal gas leak disaster ' (b) the actual or projected earning capacity of the person so afflected ; (c) the likely expenditure on immediate and anticipated medical treatment of the person so aflected ; i (d) mentalanguish and physicalinjury suffered byapersOn in the Bhopal gas ‘ leak disaster; and ‘ (e) the type and severity of physical injury suflered by the persons so affected. (5) In the event of a dispute as to disbursal of the amounts received in satisfac- ti an of claims, an appeal shall be against the order of the Deputy Commissioner to the *Add.‘ ti anal Comm ssi oner,who may decide the matter and make such disbursal as he may, for reasOns to be recorded in writing; thinkfi t.’ (6) The provisions of sub-paragraphs (6) and (7) of paragraph 5 of the Scheme shall apply to the appeal under sub-paragraph (5) as theyapply to the appeal under sub-paragraph (5) of that paragraph. ‘ 12.
